one_time_description: Gain on sale of government business enterprise (sale of 50 Class A common shares of URSA Brands Inc. / DS-LBB Holdings Inc. to Day Star First Nation)
economic_notes: First Nation operates multiple government business enterprises including LBB Gas Bar & Convenience LP, LBB Coffee Franchise LP, Wakayos Developments LP, Wakayos Holdings Inc., DS-LBB Holdings Inc., and Wakayos Employment & Training Center Inc. Sold 50% of DS-LBB Holdings Inc. (formerly URSA Brands Inc.) to Day Star First Nation in October 2020 in exchange for shares of 2544737 Ontario Limited (Consolidated Craft Brands) and $1,000,000 loan guarantee.
notes: Fiscal year is 2021 not 2024. Large one-time gain of $1,329,708 on sale of GBE (DS-LBB Holdings Inc. / URSA Brands Inc.). Six GBEs accounted for on modified equity basis. Net debt position of $7,311,632. Accumulated surplus swung from deficit of $942,516 to surplus of $1,302,546 largely due to one-time GBE sale gain. total_government_transfers calculated as ISC ($3,919,881) + FNIHB ($540,431) + CMHC subsidy ($12,295) + Western Economic Diversification ($172,828) + File Hills Health ($12,780) + Trust funds ($51,763) + Living Sky CDC ($18,882) + FHQ Tribal Council ($332,610) + YTC CFS ($237316) - some of these may be non-government; ISC and FNIHB are confirmed government. Schedule of remuneration not present in provided text. No population data disclosed.
